1--WE have 20 miles Of irrigation
ditch already dug, and this will pro-
vide water for several thousand acres
of land. More bitches are to be dug
as the public demand. Fish lake,
the source of our water supply. is
30 miles east Of here in Idaho and Is
ample to supply the needs of the en-
tire valley.


Il am astonished that so few peo-
pie as yet understand the Importance
Of this Irrigation project to Spokane.
For instance, we will be able to sup
ply water for the irrigation Of $4,000
acres of land in the valley. Poorly
irrigated 10 acres should provide a
good living for the average family.
Smaller tracts will doubtless be
taken near the city as the years go
by and the merits of the scheme are
better understood. From these figures
1t will be seen the valley will support
vast population; and, in reality, is
more important than the combined
manufacturing interests of the city.
The wealth to be taken from the
valley Is so great that it would be
hard for the average mind to compre.
bend its total.


''Our plan Is undoubtedly proving
a success, and we look for q steady
increase in business from now on
especially after the new street car
line is built up the valley to Occur
d'Alene City"
